title: The Ant and the Grasshopper
lines: In the summertime, when the weather was warm, the Grasshopper passed his days in song and merrymaking.
The Ant, meanwhile, worked day and night, storing up grain and provisions for the winter.
"Why do you work so hard?" asked the Grasshopper.
"Come and enjoy yourself with me; there is plenty of time to work."
But the Ant went on with his labor, making no reply.
When winter came, the Grasshopper found himself dying of hunger, while the Ant was distributing corn and grain from his well-stored larder.
"Why did you not work in summer?" asked the Ant.
"I sang and made merry," replied the Grasshopper.
moral: Hard work leads to success; idleness to failure.
